reasoning: XGBoost is the dominant architecture (best MAE 20.59, beats LightGBM by 8+ MAE points), but both XGBoost variants share the same structural weakness: folds 1-2 achieve MAE ~17 while folds 4-5 plateau at ~22-24, a 35% temporal degradation that flags overfitting on early time windows. Mathematical analysis of the two existing XGBoost models proves their optimal static weighted blend converges to w=1 on the champion (fold-mean MAE is linear in blend weight), making a weighted ensemble of these two provably worthless. With exactly 1 experiment remaining, explicit regularization — depth reduction plus gamma/L1/L2/min_child_weight — is the only remaining high-confidence lever to close the late-fold gap.

propuestas.descripcion: XGBoost with depth 7→5 plus gamma/L1/L2/min_child_weight to close the fold 4-5 temporal overfitting gradient
propuestas.evidencia: Both XGBoost runs use max_depth=7 with zero explicit regularization (gamma=0, reg_alpha=0, reg_lambda=1 default, min_child_weight=1 default). Fold pattern [17.64, 16.95, 22.04, 23.83, 22.48] is a textbook temporal overfitting signature — MAE climbs 35% from early to late folds as the model struggles to generalize beyond its training window. Depth 7→5 is the primary lever: it cuts maximum leaf-level memorization by ~4×. gamma=0.3 raises the minimum-gain threshold for any split, pruning low-signal nodes. reg_alpha=0.5 applies L1 sparsity to noisy feature interactions. reg_lambda=2.0 doubles default L2 shrinkage. min_child_weight=3 prevents splits on thin seasonal nodes with few observations. n_estimators bumped to 400 to compensate for the lower learning rate and regularization-induced shrinkage. A weighted ensemble of the two existing XGBoost models is ruled out by algebra: mean MAE = 20.722 − 0.134·w, which is strictly minimized at w=1 (full weight to champion), yielding exactly 20.59 with zero gain from blending.

propuestas.descripcion: Stacking ensemble of XGB champion + XGB runner-up with ridge meta-learner exploiting fold-specific complementarity
propuestas.evidencia: XGB2 dominates folds 1-2 (15.88, 15.32) while XGB1 dominates folds 3-5 (22.04, 23.83, 22.48 vs 22.50, 25.95, 23.96), a fold-specific complementarity a ridge meta-learner could theoretically exploit to assign time-adaptive weights. However, the meta-learner trains on only 5 fold-level observations — far too few to learn reliable fold-conditional weights without overfitting the meta-level itself. Expected gain is marginal at best. NOT recommended as the final experiment: xgboost_regularized_depth_v3 has 8× higher expected score and directly attacks the root cause.
